verses.text: Woe to the rebellious children who take counsel but not of God, going down to Egypt for help. They trust man, not the LORD. To seek safety apart from God only adds sin to shame.
Woe to the rebellious children who take counsel but not of God, going down to Egypt for help. They trust man, not the LORD. To seek safety apart from God only adds sin to shame.
Woe to the rebellious children who take counsel but not of God, going down to Egypt for help. They trust man, not the LORD. To seek safety apart from God only adds sin to shame.
Their envoys reach Egypt, but her help is 'in vain, and to no purpose'; her strength is 'to sit still.' Egypt cannot save. Those who lean on the world find their props are empty.
Their envoys reach Egypt, but her help is 'in vain, and to no purpose'; her strength is 'to sit still.' Egypt cannot save. Those who lean on the world find their props are empty.

God bids Isaiah write that this is a rebellious people who say, 'Prophesy not unto us right things... speak unto us smooth things.' They want flattery, not truth. The heart in rebellion silences the voice that would correct it.

God bids Isaiah write that this is a rebellious people who say, 'Prophesy not unto us right things... speak unto us smooth things.' They want flattery, not truth. The heart in rebellion silences the voice that would correct it.

Because they despise God's word and trust oppression, their sin shall be 'as a breach ready to fall,' shattered suddenly like a potter's vessel. Rejected warning brings ruin. The crack ignored becomes a sudden collapse.

'In returning and rest shall ye be saved; in quietness and in confidence shall be your strength: and ye would not.' Salvation lay in quiet trust. They chose flight on swift horses, and so were put to flight.

'In returning and rest shall ye be saved; in quietness and in confidence shall be your strength: and ye would not.' Salvation lay in quiet trust. They chose flight on swift horses, and so were put to flight.

'In returning and rest shall ye be saved; in quietness and in confidence shall be your strength: and ye would not.' Salvation lay in quiet trust. They chose flight on swift horses, and so were put to flight.
Yet the LORD waits to be gracious, 'blessed are all they that wait for him'; He will answer the cry of His people. His patience invites return. God lingers in mercy even over a stubborn people.
Yet the LORD waits to be gracious, 'blessed are all they that wait for him'; He will answer the cry of His people. His patience invites return. God lingers in mercy even over a stubborn people.

Though He gives the bread of adversity, their Teacher shall not hide, and they shall hear a voice, 'This is the way, walk ye in it,' and cast away their idols. Affliction with guidance is mercy. God directs the steps of those He chastens.

Then He will send rain for the seed, rich pasture, and light so bright the moon shines as the sun, in the day He binds up His people's wound. Blessing follows healing. God restores abundance to those He has chastened and healed.

The name of the LORD comes from afar, burning with anger, His tongue 'as a devouring fire,' while His people sing as on a holy night. Judgment and joy meet. The same coming that terrifies the wicked gladdens the faithful.

By the voice of the LORD the Assyrian shall be beaten down, for 'Tophet is ordained of old,' a pyre prepared for the proud king. His doom is fixed. The God who threatens His people's foes has already prepared their end.
